content.problemStatement:
**Special Sum** of **4** numbers **(a, b, c, d)** is defined as:

**|a+b-c-d| + |a+c-b-d| + |a+d-b-c| + |c+d-a-b| + |b+d-a-c| + |b+c-a-d|**
where **|x|** denotes absolute value of **x**.

Given an array **A** of size **N**, you need to find the sum of **Special Sum** of numbers taken over all quadruples of different indices of the array.
constraints: 1 ≤ Sum of N over all test case ≤ 2000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains the integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the sum of the Special Sum of numbers taken over all quadruples of different indices of the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the Special Sum is calculated as follows: |1+2-3-3| + |1+3-2-3| + |1+3-2-3| + |3+3-1-2| + |2+3-1-3| + |2+3-1-3| = 2 + 1 + 1 + 3 + 2 + 2 = 10.
For this test case, all elements are equal, so the Special Sum is 0.
For this test case, the Special Sum is calculated as follows: |1+2-3-4| + |1+3-2-4| + |1+4-2-3| + |3+4-1-2| + |2+4-1-3| + |2+3-1-4| + ... = 76.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the Special Sum for all possible quadruples of different indices in the array. We can use six nested loops to generate all possible quadruples and calculate the Special Sum for each quadruple. The time complexity of this approach is O(T*N^4) because we have four nested loops to generate all possible quadruples and one loop to iterate over all test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N^4) — because we have four nested loops to generate all possible quadruples and one loop to iterate over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and the output
